text: On April 8th, "Lost" fans celebrated a significant date, but it was also "Rex Manning Day" honoring the film Empire Records. Other notable dates from movies include April 14th (Titanic), April 25th (Miss Congeniality), October 3rd (Mean Girls), and October 21st (Back to the Future Part II).

The original manuscript for Don McLean's "American Pie" has sold at auction for $1.2m, achieving the third highest auction price for an American literary manuscript. The 16-page draft includes 237 lines of manuscript and 26 lines of typed text, some of which didn't make it to the final song and extensive notes. McLean, who turns 70 this year, said that it was time to part with the manuscript to benefit his wife and two children as none of them have the "mercantile instinct".

text: A giraffe named Katie from the Dallas Zoo gave birth to a 6-foot tall calf, streamed live by Animal Planet. Details of the newborn's gender or condition were not immediately available, but initial signs indicated good health. The newborn joins a 4-year-old sister, Jamie. Katie's labor was watched by fellow giraffes from a nearby barn, including her best friend, Jade. The Dallas Zoo refers to Katie as the "diva" among the zoo's 12 giraffes.

The birth was witnessed by viewers on Animal Planet and the Dallas Zoo's Twitter feed. The baby's gender and condition were not immediately known, but there were positive signs of movement and nursing.
